title: A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Internal Revenue Service relating to "Gross Proceeds Reporting by Brokers That Regularly Provide Services Effectuating Digital Asset Sales".
stage_label: Senate Committee on Finance discharged, by petition, pursuant to 5 U.S.C. 802(c).
plain_summary: This bill stops a new IRS rule that was made on December 30, 2024. The rule would have required people who help with digital money transactions to report information about sales to the IRS. This includes people who work with decentralized finance, which is a type of digital money system. Congress voted to reject this rule, so it will not go into effect. This means these reporting requirements will not happen.
last_action_text: Message received in Senate: Returned to the Senate pursuant to the provisions of H.Res. 212.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress has decided to reject a new rule made by the Internal Revenue Service. The rule is called Gross Proceeds Reporting by Brokers That Regularly Provide Services Effectuating Digital Asset Sales. This rule was published in the Federal Register on December thirtieth, two thousand twenty four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no power and cannot be enforced. The Senate passed this joint resolution on March fourth, two thousand twenty five.
